sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: Good, clicking the purple ring restored the white star. Now let me check where the orange square is and what the white star looks like.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['pixels'] == 20:
        rs = [x for x,_ in n['boundary']]
        cs = [y for _,y in n['boundary']]
        center = ((min(rs)+max(rs))//2, (min(cs)+max(cs))//2)
        print(f"Square color={n['color']} center={center}")

for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'W' and n['pixels'] < 20:
        rs = [x for x,_ in n['boundary']]
        cs = [y for _,y in n['boundary']]
        center = ((min(rs)+max(rs))//2, (min(cs)+max(cs))//2)
        children = [seg['nodes'][c]['color'] for c in n['children']] if n['children'] else []
        print(f"White px={n['pixels']} center={center} children={children}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

In level 1, after clicking the ring, I clicked the square to move it toward the ring. Let me try clicking the orange square now to see if it moves toward the white star at (18, 57).
The orange square is at (16, 38), and the white star is at (18, 57). If the square moves toward the white star, it should move right (increasing column) and slightly down (increasing row).

sections.content:

</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
